title: Houses for Rent in Stockyards, Cleveland
metaTitle: Houses for Rent in Stockyards, Cleveland OH
metaDescription: Looking for houses for rent in Stockyards, Cleveland? See rent ranges, transit access, and Section 8 basics, then contact us about nearby west-side homes.
secondaryKeywords: near west side cleveland houses for rent
answerBox: Stockyards is a near-west side Cleveland neighborhood adjacent to Clark-Fulton, named for the livestock yards historically located there. Citywide, Zumper's July 2026 report puts Cleveland's median asking rent for a 2-bedroom at $1,100 and a 3-bedroom at $1,350; contact us to tell us your needs near Stockyards and nearby west-side ZIP codes.

body: Stockyards is a near-west side Cleveland neighborhood adjacent to Clark-Fulton, taking its name from the livestock stockyards historically located in the area. The surrounding blocks are made up largely of early-1900s single-family homes and duplexes on narrow lots typical of Cleveland's west side, mixed with light-industrial buildings that reflect the neighborhood's stockyard-era history.

We're upfront that our own rental listings aren't concentrated in Stockyards specifically. Our portfolio is currently concentrated on Cleveland's east and southeast side — neighborhoods like Slavic Village, Collinwood, Glenville, and Hough — with a smaller number of homes on the west side in nearby ZIP codes like 44102 (Cudell/Detroit-Shoreway) and 44113 (Ohio City/Tremont).

If you're looking for a rental home near Stockyards, [contact our team](/p/book-showing) and tell us your bedroom count, budget, and timeline — we'll let you know what we can offer in the area, including our nearby [Clark-Fulton listings](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-clark-fulton-cleveland/) just north of Stockyards.

sections.h2: How much does it cost to rent a house near Stockyards?
body: We don't have enough of our own listings directly in Stockyards to publish a neighborhood-specific rent range with confidence. For general orientation, Zumper's July 4, 2026 report puts Cleveland's citywide median asking rent at **$1,250/mo**, with 2-bedroom units averaging **$1,100** and 3-bedrooms averaging **$1,350**. RentCafe's July 2, 2026 report, which tracks larger professionally-managed apartment buildings, shows a higher citywide average of **$1,818** for a 2-bedroom and **$2,716** for a 3-bedroom — the gap reflects the difference between smaller rental houses and newer managed apartment complexes.

Stockyards is adjacent to Clark-Fulton, the near-west side corridor along Clark Avenue and W. 25th Street that includes the MetroHealth main campus. RTA bus routes run along Clark Avenue and connect toward downtown Cleveland, and the neighborhood is a short drive from I-71.

Downtown Cleveland is roughly a 10-minute drive northeast via I-71 or Clark Avenue, and the neighborhood's proximity to MetroHealth's main campus makes it a common stop for RTA routes serving hospital shifts and appointments.

items: **Location:** Near-west side, directly adjacent to Clark-Fulton
**Transit:** RTA bus routes along Clark Avenue toward downtown
**Nearby institutions:** MetroHealth main campus, just north in Clark-Fulton

body: Every home we manage anywhere in Greater Cleveland accepts Section 8 / Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready, though we don't currently have listings we can point to directly in Stockyards. Ohio has no statewide source-of-income protection law, and the City of Cleveland does not require landlords to accept vouchers; only Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, University Heights, Warrensville Heights, and Linndale have local ordinances that do. Apply for a voucher through [CMHA](https://www.cmha.net), and see our full [Cleveland Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for the details.

sections.h2: What if I want to rent near Stockyards but you don't have a listing there?
body: Tell us anyway. Because we manage homes throughout Greater Cleveland, including nearby west-side ZIP codes, a home just outside Stockyards' boundaries in Clark-Fulton, Cudell, or Ohio City/Tremont may fit what you need even if it isn't inside the Stockyards SPA itself. [Contact our team](/p/book-showing) with your bedroom count, budget, and whether a voucher is involved, and we'll follow up with anything that matches.

As with any home we manage, applications go through our online **DoorLoop** portal, and standard screening applies to every applicant with no shortcuts around income or background checks. We do welcome vouchers and will consider references or a co-signer if your credit history is limited. See our broader [houses for rent in Cleveland](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) page for the full list of areas we currently serve.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland have houses for rent in Stockyards?
faq.a: Our current portfolio is concentrated on Cleveland's east and southeast side, with a smaller west-side presence in nearby ZIP codes. We may not have a listing directly in Stockyards at any given time. Contact us with your bedroom count and budget and we'll tell you what's available nearby.

faq.q: How much does it cost to rent a house near Stockyards?
faq.a: We don't have enough listings in Stockyards specifically to publish a local range. Citywide, Zumper's July 2026 report shows a 2-bedroom median of $1,100 and a 3-bedroom median of $1,350 for Cleveland overall.

faq.q: Where is Stockyards in relation to other Cleveland neighborhoods?
faq.a: Stockyards is a near-west side Cleveland neighborhood directly adjacent to Clark-Fulton, near the MetroHealth main campus corridor along Clark Avenue and W. 25th Street.

faq.q: Does every home you manage accept Section 8 vouchers?
faq.a: Yes, every home in our portfolio accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready, regardless of neighborhood. Keep in mind the City of Cleveland does not legally require any landlord to accept a voucher, since Ohio has no statewide source-of-income protection law.

faq.q: Can I still apply if the home I want is just outside Stockyards?
faq.a: Yes. We manage homes in nearby west-side ZIP codes like Clark-Fulton, Cudell/Detroit-Shoreway (44102), and Ohio City/Tremont (44113), along with a larger concentration on the east and southeast side. Contact us with your bedroom count and budget and we'll point you to anything that fits, whether or not it's inside Stockyards' exact boundaries.
